req.title: Break-glass access (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall invoke break-glass access for an emergent patient; break-glass use shall be audited and trigger a post-event review. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.
preconditions: role:clinician
patient exists

doc.reasoning: The break-glass endpoint has three primary test axes: (1) a valid clinician invoking emergency access for an existing patient (positive/EP), (2) negative paths covering missing mandatory fields, wrong role, and conflicting state transitions (NEG/DT), and (3) boundary conditions such as a reason field at exactly the minimum and maximum allowed length (BVA). State transition coverage is exercised by attempting a second break-glass invocation when one is already active for the same patient (STC). Decision table covers the cross-product of role x patient-exists x reason-present. Each test is self-contained via fixture teardown and uses idempotent patient/session identifiers.

tests.name: test_break_glass_valid_clinician_grants_access_and_emits_audit
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role=clinician
Patient record with known patient_id exists and is in normal state
No active break-glass session exists for this patient
actions: POST /BreakGlass with valid clinician token, patient_id, and non-empty emergency_reason
Capture HTTP response status and body
Query audit log endpoint for the emitted event
expected: HTTP 201 Created
Response body contains access_token for the patient record
Response body contains break_glass_id
Audit log contains one event with action=BREAK_GLASS_ACCESS and actor matching clinician id
Audit event timestamp is within 5 seconds of request time
trace: R-HLT-071: clinician invokes break-glass for emergent patient
R-HLT-071: break-glass use shall be audited
tests.executable:
def test_break_glass_valid_clinician_grants_access_and_emits_audit(
    clinician_session, existing_patient_id, audit_log_client, cleanup_break_glass
):
    payload = {
        "patient_id": existing_patient_id,
        "emergency_reason": "Patient unresponsive in ED"
    }
    resp = clinician_session.post("/BreakGlass", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "access_token" in body
    assert "break_glass_id" in body
    cleanup_break_glass(body["break_glass_id"])

    events = audit_log_client.fetch(
        action="BREAK_GLASS_ACCESS",
        resource_id=existing_patient_id
    )
    assert len(events) >= 1
    evt = events[-1]
    assert evt["actor_id"] == clinician_session.user_id
    assert <within_seconds>(evt["timestamp"], seconds=5)

tests.name: test_break_glass_missing_emergency_reason_returns_400
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role=clinician
Patient record with known patient_id exists
actions: POST /BreakGlass with valid clinician token and patient_id but omit emergency_reason field entirely
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 400 Bad Request
Response body error code equals MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELD
Response body identifies emergency_reason as the missing field
No audit event is emitted for this request
trace: R-HLT-071: missing mandatory elements must be tested (negative path)
tests.executable:
def test_break_glass_missing_emergency_reason_returns_400(
    clinician_session, existing_patient_id, audit_log_client
):
    payload = {"patient_id": existing_patient_id}
    resp = clinician_session.post("/BreakGlass", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELD"
    assert "emergency_reason" in body.get("fields", [])

    events = audit_log_client.fetch(
        action="BREAK_GLASS_ACCESS",
        resource_id=existing_patient_id,
        since=<now_minus_seconds>(10)
    )
    assert len(events) == 0

tests.name: test_break_glass_non_clinician_role_returns_403
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role=billing_admin (not clinician)
Patient record with known patient_id exists
actions: POST /BreakGlass with billing_admin token, patient_id, and valid emergency_reason
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 403 Forbidden
Response body error code equals PERMISSION_DENIED
No break_glass_id is returned
No audit event emitted for BREAK_GLASS_ACCESS
trace: R-HLT-071: permission denial must be tested (negative path)
R-HLT-071: actors restricted to clinician role
tests.executable:
def test_break_glass_non_clinician_role_returns_403(
    billing_admin_session, existing_patient_id, audit_log_client
):
    payload = {
        "patient_id": existing_patient_id,
        "emergency_reason": "Unauthorized role attempt"
    }
    resp = billing_admin_session.post("/BreakGlass", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "PERMISSION_DENIED"
    assert "break_glass_id" not in body

    events = audit_log_client.fetch(
        action="BREAK_GLASS_ACCESS",
        resource_id=existing_patient_id,
        since=<now_minus_seconds>(10)
    )
    assert len(events) == 0

tests.name: test_break_glass_duplicate_active_session_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role=clinician
Patient record with known patient_id exists
An active break-glass session already exists for this patient (state=ACTIVE)
actions: POST /BreakGlass a first time to establish ACTIVE state; record break_glass_id
POST /BreakGlass a second time with the same patient_id while first session is ACTIVE
Capture HTTP response status and body for the second request
expected: First POST returns HTTP 201
Second POST returns HTTP 409 Conflict
Response body error code equals CONFLICTING_STATE_TRANSITION
Only one audit event exists for BREAK_GLASS_ACCESS for this patient in this window
trace: R-HLT-071: conflicting state transitions must be tested (negative path)
R-HLT-071: state transition coverage NORMAL -> ACTIVE -> ACTIVE (invalid)
tests.executable:
def test_break_glass_duplicate_active_session_returns_409(
    clinician_session, existing_patient_id, audit_log_client, cleanup_break_glass
):
    payload = {
        "patient_id": existing_patient_id,
        "emergency_reason": "First emergency invocation"
    }
    first = clinician_session.post("/BreakGlass", json=payload)
    assert first.status_code == 201
    bg_id = first.json()["break_glass_id"]
    cleanup_break_glass(bg_id)

    second = clinician_session.post("/BreakGlass", json=payload)
    assert second.status_code == 409
    body = second.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "CONFLICTING_STATE_TRANSITION"

    events = audit_log_client.fetch(
        action="BREAK_GLASS_ACCESS",
        resource_id=existing_patient_id,
        since=<now_minus_seconds>(30)
    )
    assert len(events) == 1

tests.name: test_break_glass_emergency_reason_at_minimum_length_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role=clinician
Patient record with known patient_id exists
No active break-glass session for this patient
API documentation states emergency_reason minimum length is 10 characters
actions: POST /BreakGlass with emergency_reason of exactly 10 characters (minimum valid length)
Capture HTTP response status and body
POST /BreakGlass with emergency_reason of exactly 9 characters (one below minimum)
Capture HTTP response status and body for second request
expected: 10-character reason: HTTP 201 Created with break_glass_id present
9-character reason: HTTP 400 Bad Request with error_code VALIDATION_ERROR
Audit event emitted only for the successful 10-character request
trace: R-HLT-071: mandatory element validation — emergency_reason minimum length boundary
R-HLT-071: missing/invalid mandatory elements must be tested
tests.executable:
def test_break_glass_emergency_reason_at_minimum_length_boundary(
    clinician_session, existing_patient_id, audit_log_client, cleanup_break_glass
):
    MIN_LEN = 10
    reason_valid = "A" * MIN_LEN
    reason_short = "A" * (MIN_LEN - 1)

    resp_valid = clinician_session.post("/BreakGlass", json={
        "patient_id": existing_patient_id,
        "emergency_reason": reason_valid
    })
    assert resp_valid.status_code == 201
    bg_id = resp_valid.json().get("break_glass_id")
    assert bg_id is not None
    cleanup_break_glass(bg_id)
    <close_break_glass_session>(clinician_session, bg_id)

    resp_short = clinician_session.post("/BreakGlass", json={
        "patient_id": existing_patient_id,
        "emergency_reason": reason_short
    })
    assert resp_short.status_code == 400
    assert resp_short.json().get("error_code") == "VALIDATION_ERROR"

    events = audit_log_client.fetch(
        action="BREAK_GLASS_ACCESS",
        resource_id=existing_patient_id,
        since=<now_minus_seconds>(30)
    )
    assert len(events) == 1

tests.name: test_break_glass_post_event_review_task_created
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role=clinician
Patient record with known patient_id exists
No active break-glass session for this patient
Review task service is reachable
actions: POST /BreakGlass with valid clinician token, patient_id, and emergency_reason
Query review task service for tasks linked to break_glass_id
expected: HTTP 201 Created
At least one review task exists with status=PENDING linked to break_glass_id
Review task contains clinician actor_id and patient_id
Review task type equals POST_BREAK_GLASS_REVIEW
trace: R-HLT-071: break-glass use shall trigger a post-event review
R-HLT-071: error pathways — review task created
tests.executable:
def test_break_glass_post_event_review_task_created(
    clinician_session, existing_patient_id, review_task_client, cleanup_break_glass
):
    payload = {
        "patient_id": existing_patient_id,
        "emergency_reason": "Cardiac arrest, immediate access needed"
    }
    resp = clinician_session.post("/BreakGlass", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    bg_id = resp.json()["break_glass_id"]
    cleanup_break_glass(bg_id)

    tasks = review_task_client.fetch_by_break_glass_id(bg_id)
    assert len(tasks) >= 1
    task = tasks[0]
    assert task["status"] == "PENDING"
    assert task["task_type"] == "POST_BREAK_GLASS_REVIEW"
    assert task["actor_id"] == clinician_session.user_id
    assert task["patient_id"] == existing_patient_id
